content: Phase 1 Survey: AgenticAKM is an academic research prototype (ICSE 2025 paper) for agentic architecture knowledge management. Python 3.12, Jupyter notebooks, google-generativeai (Gemini 2.5 Pro), openai (GPT-5), pydantic. ~646 LOC in AdrAgents.py, ~1500-2000 LOC across 5 notebooks. 1,446 generated ADRs across 41 repos. No tests, no CI/CD, no requirements.txt. Key patterns identified: (1) Generate-Verify-Regenerate Loop with feedback injection, (2) Save-Last deferred persistence, (3) Structured output with Pydantic validation + graceful fallback, (4) Verifier with CORRECT/INCORRECT verdict protocol. Anti-patterns: file-size-based selection heuristic, no tests, global mutable state, soft failure boundaries, no temp dir cleanup.

Two applicable patterns found. (1) Generate-verify-regenerate loop applicable to /analyze-project Phase 1 survey — facilitator maintains loop, not subagent. Survey output gets quality gate before specialist dispatch. (2) Save-last pattern already implicitly followed in artifact generation (review reports); making explicit prevents partial artifact contamination on interrupted sessions. Dissent: Loop NOT applicable to /build_module where pytest already provides deterministic verification.

Two applicable patterns found. (1) Pydantic-validated structured output schema for ADR fields addresses real gap: no machine-checkable ADR completeness validation. Lightweight ADR validator checking required YAML frontmatter fields and markdown sections would catch malformed ADRs before entering docs/adr/. (2) Empirical evidence that agentic decomposition significantly outperforms single-LLM calls for architecture documentation quality (4.5/5 vs 3.5/5) — evidence that /analyze-project single-pass survey phase may leave completeness on the table.

Most underappreciated finding: empirical completeness quality gap (Agent Gemini: 4.0/5 vs LLM Gemini: 2.5/5). Our /analyze-project has no quality gate on Phase 1 project profile — if survey is incomplete, all specialist evaluations inherit that error. Dissent with architecture-consultant: generate-verify-regenerate should NOT be applied to /build_module where pytest already verifies. Pattern value is highest where deterministic verification is unavailable — exactly /analyze-project survey (no ground truth) and ADR generation (no test suite). This is 2nd sighting of decomposed pipeline principle (1st: Swarm Plan-Execute-Review, DEFERRED).

Synthesis of AgenticAKM analysis. 3 specialists dispatched (architecture-consultant, docs-knowledge, independent-perspective). 4 patterns evaluated. Convergence: all 3 agree Phase 1 survey in /analyze-project lacks quality gate; save-last is implicitly followed but undocumented. Dissent: architecture-consultant proposed loop for /build_module, independent-perspective correctly argued pytest already verifies there. Blind spots: cost accounting, file selection heuristic (our approach is already superior). Scoring: (1) Survey Quality Gate 19/25 DEFER - high value but needs design work; (2) Save-Last Documentation 16/25 DEFER; (3) ADR Completeness Validator 20/25 ADOPT; (4) Verifier Verdict Protocol 14/25 SKIP. Adoption log update: generate-verify-regenerate is 2nd sighting of Swarm Plan-Execute-Review principle.
